据Carlo Zorzoli的Enel Green Power，今天氢的生产是一种“气候杀手”。
 他说，一些“98％的它是由蒸汽重整和气化产生的，这相当于与印度尼西亚和英国相当的年度碳排放量相同。只有2％的电解产生。“
 “今天，氢气只是干净。今天生产的98％是工业原料。只有2％的电解产生。“
 “今天的氢不是脱碳的溶液：氢是问题的一部分。所以第一件事要做的是将灰色氢转化为绿色。“
  Zorzoli，Zorzoli是Enel Green Power的业务发展主管，在一个旨在“缩放可再生和碳中性的可再生能源的可再生能源”的Zorzoli，他强调了Enel的信念，即“电气化是迄今为止最便宜和最简单的方式脱碳我们的经济“。
  但他承认“当然你不能通过一切 - 所以需要一些东西来补充电气化。绿色氢肯定是一个补充。“
 为了使它成为“绿色”，他表示，这是能源领域的优先事项，以降低电解师的成本。 
“因为不可蓄水部门的未来主要依赖于氢或氢基载体。 我们需要专注于使绿色氢更便宜。“  他说，欧洲的脱碳之路是“我们正在进行的一项大任务”，并补充说，Enel Green Power在四大主要趋势下拥有可持续的商业模式：脱碳，电气化; 数字化; 和新的客户需求。  在解决围绕所需的问题时，更改以加速更好的可再生能源的部署，他说：“我们看到允许缓慢的流程 - 以及提供足够许可证的缺乏能力。 我们肯定有一个问题 - 有一个问题，许可证，在我看来，大多数国家的允许是一个瓶颈。“
note_en:
The production of hydrogen today is a “climate killer” according to Carlo Zorzoli of Enel Green Power.
 He said some “98% of it is produced from steam reforming and gasification, which equates to yearly carbon emissions comparable to that of Indonesia and the UK combined. Just 2% is produced from electrolysis.”
 “Today, hydrogen is anything but clean. That 98% produced today is an industrial feedstock. Just 2% is produced from electrolysis.”
 “Hydrogen today is not a solution to decarbonisation: hydrogen is a part of the problem. So the very first thing to do is convert grey hydrogen to green.”
  Zorzoli, who is head of business development at Enel Green Power, was speaking in an Enlit Europe webcast titled ‘Scaling up renewables for smart electrification and carbon neutrality ’, in which he stressed Enel’s belief that  “electrification is by far the cheapest and simplest way to decarbonise our economies”.
  But he acknowledged that “of course you cannot electrify everything – and so there is a need for something to complement electrification. And green hydrogen is definitely a complement.”
 And to make it ‘green’, he said it was a priority for the energy sector to bring down the cost of electrolysers.
  “Because the future for the not-electrifiable sector relies mostly on hydrogen or hydrogen-based vectors. We need to focus on making green hydrogen cheaper.”
 He said Europe’s road to decarbonisation was “a big task we’re on” and added that Enel Green Power had orgainised its sustainable business model around four major trends: decarbonisation, electrification; digitalisation; and new customer needs.
  In addressing a question around what needed to change to speed up a greater deployment of renewables, he said: “We see a slow permitting process – and a lack of capability to deliver enough permits. We definitely have a problem – there is an issue of timing with permits and, in my view, permitting in most countries is a bottleneck.”
title: 氢生产是一个“气候杀手”伊尔·老板说
title_en: Hydrogen production is a ‘climate killer’ says Enel boss

新闻自由指数是根据本组织自身评估的国家新闻自由结果，记者每年发布的180个国家的排名。这种排名旨在反映每个国家的记者，媒体和网民的自由程度，以及当局尊重这一自由的努力。
 世界媒体自由指数由许多指标组成，即：
 媒体独立性：衡量媒体能够独立于政治，政府，商业和宗教权力和影响力的程度来运作的程度。
  立法框架：衡量立法框架管理新闻和信息活动的影响。
 透明度：衡量影响新闻和信息的制作的机构和程序的透明度。
 基础设施：测量支持新闻和信息的基础设施的质量。
 滥用行为：在计算期间，基于收集的数据基于收集的数据和对记者和媒体的暴力行为的行为也被考虑在计算中。 
最终得分在0到100的范围内定义。一个国家的分数越高，该国家的情况越令人担忧。新闻自由地图介绍了世界排名180个国家的表现的视觉摘要。
       尽管某些国家的压抑性政策，欧洲仍然是最有利于新闻自由的大陆。虽然有几个国家是尊重新闻自由的最佳状态，但欧盟越来越多的异质，受到连续经济危机的破坏，反对恐怖主义的斗争，而且还由越来越多地向更自主的制度滑动。
   新闻自由指数的顶级和底部在北部和中美洲排名：
  在北美，虽然国家历史地尊重新闻界，但这些国家的记者受到他们报告的机构的挑战。记者和媒体定期面临口头和身体威胁，否认获取信息，因为限制其权利的法律和诉讼。
     在南美洲，十年内始于新闻自由情况的一般恶化。拉丁美洲记者经营的背景日益复杂，敌对，几个国家是世界上最危险的记者的地方。
     非洲正在录制重大挫折，特别是由于长期任意暗示的增加，也是为了攻击，特别是在线和新的镇压法，这些法律以反对不忠实或网络犯罪的名义，可以习惯于不合理地限制信息自由。媒体的经济疲软也使他们暴露于对其独立性有害的政治或经济影响。
     亚洲地区，为该职业最危险的多危险国家统计，汇集了世界上最大的记者和博主的监狱。亚太地区还拥有大量的“新闻自由”国家，真正的信息黑洞。 
这2020年世界新闻自由指数指出，由于五个会聚因子，未来十年将是新闻自由的决定性：  这五种类型的危机现在增加了健康危机。 实际上，冠状病毒大流行突出显示并放大了这些多个危机，今天威胁到自由，独立，多元化和可靠的信息。
note_en:
The Press Freedom Index is a ranking of 180 countries published yearly by Reporters Without Borders on the basis of countries’ press freedom results according to the organization’s own assessment. This ranking attempts to reflect the degree of freedom that journalists, media, and netizens have in each country, and the efforts made by the authorities to respect this freedom.
 The World Press Freedom Index is composed of a number of indicators, which are :
 Media independence : Measures the degree to which the media are able to function independently of sources of political, governmental, business and religious power and influence.
  Legislative framework : Measures the impact of the legislative framework governing news and information activities.
 Transparency : Measures the transparency of the institutions and procedures that affect the production of news and information.
 Infrastructure : Measures the quality of the infrastructure that supports the production of news and information.
 Abuses : A seventh indicator based on data gathered about abuses and acts of violence against journalists and media during the period evaluated is also factored into the calculation.
 The final score is defined on a scale from 0 to 100. The higher the score of a country, the more worrying the situation for that country. The press freedom map presents a visual summary of the performance of 180 countries in the World Ranking.
       Europe remains the continent the most in favor of press freedom, despite the repressive policies of certain countries. Although several states are among those which respect press freedom the best, the EU is increasingly heterogeneous, undermined by the successive economic crises, the fight against terrorism, but also by states sliding more and more towards a more authoritarian regime.
   Top and bottom of the Press Freedom Index ranking in North and Central America :
  In North America, though nations have historically respected the press, journalists in these countries are being challenged by the very institutions on which they report. Journalists and the media regularly face verbal and physical threats, denied access to information, as laws and lawsuits restricting their rights multiply.
     In South America, the decade begins with a general deterioration in the situation of press freedom. The context in which journalists in Latin America operate is increasingly complex and hostile, and several countries are among the world’s most dangerous places for journalists.
     Africa is recording significant setbacks, due in particular to an increase in long-term arbitrary detentions, but also to attacks, particularly online, and new repressive laws which, in the name of the fight against disinformation or cybercrime, can be used to unreasonably restrict freedom of information. The economic weakness of the media also exposes them to political or economic influences detrimental to their independence.
     The Asia zone, counts manifold countries among the most dangerous for the profession and brings together the largest prisons in the world for journalists and bloggers. The Asia-Pacific region also holds a great number of “predators of press freedom” states, true black holes of information.
  This 2020 World Press Freedom Index states that the coming decade is going to be decisive for press freedom due to five converging factors which can be :
  To these five types of crisis is now added a health crisis. Indeed, the coronavirus pandemic highlights and amplifies these multiple crises which today threaten the right to free, independent, pluralistic and reliable information.
title: 2020年世界媒体自由指数：新闻未来的决定性十年
title_en: 2020 World Press Freedom Index: A decisive decade for the future of journalism
